ground_truth.title: Top 5 Cheapest Software Development Degrees Online
ground_truth.main_content: Whether you want to start your own business, or work for a major tech company, an online degree in software development can get you there. Software development is one of the fastest growing careers in the United States. It's a lucrative profession with a massive range of options, and all you need to get your career going is a bachelor's degree. Our ranking features five of the most affordable online software development degrees in the United States. #1. Western Governors University - Online Bachelor of Science in Software Development. The cheap online degree in software development at Western Governors University offers both Java and C# tracks. Most students enter this program in order to complete a degree they already started and 83% of graduates finish in six months to a year. The tuition is charged per term, so you save money the more courses you take. Tuition: $6,380. #2. Eastern Florida State College - Online Bachelor of Applied Science in Software Development. Students looking to enter must complete calculus 1, general physics 1, and a C++ programming course with a grade of C or higher. Resident Tuition: $2,496. Non-resident Tuition: $9,739. #3. American Intercontinental University - Online Bachelor of Science in IT: Software Analysis and Development. Students will study subject areas like e-commerce development, software quality control, and software development for mobile. Tuition: $11,425. #4. University of Maryland Global Campus - Bachelor of Science in Software Development and Security Online. The low-cost online software development degree is unique in that it combines software development and security. Resident Tuition: $7,296. Non-resident Tuition: $12,336. #5. Bellevue University - Online Bachelor of Science in Software Development. The cheap online software development degree covers programming with Java and Ruby on Rails, SQL development, web application programming, and project management. Tuition: $12,450. FAQs cover career options, job outlook, financing options, and whether online learning is right for you.
with: Whether you want to start your own business, or work for a major tech company, an online degree in software development can get you there.
Western Governors University offers both Java and C# tracks and 83% of graduates finish in six months to a year.
The low-cost online software development degree at UMGC is unique in that it combines software development and security.
Software developers have a 24% job growth rate between 2016 and 2026 according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics.
Federal student loans also provide you with a lot of flexibility when it comes to paying them back, with multiple incredible options depending on your financial circumstances.
